author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
title: The Opposing Passages.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And yet the passages are true which he goes on to adduce by way of answer, saying: “The Saviour in the gospel declares, ‘Blessed are the pure in heart; for they shall see God.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: David also says, ‘Who shall ascend into the hill of the Lord? or who shall stand in His holy place?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He that is innocent in his hands, and pure in his heart;’ and again in another passage, ‘Do good, O Lord, unto those that be good and upright in heart.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So also in Solomon: ‘Riches are good unto him that hath no sin on his conscience;’ and again in the same book, ‘Leave off from sin, and order thine hands aright, and cleanse thy heart from wickedness.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So in the Epistle of John, ‘If our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God; and whatsoever we ask, we shall receive of Him.’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For all this is accomplished by the will, by the exercise of faith, hope, and love; by keeping under the body; by doing alms; by forgiving injuries; by earnest prayer; by supplicating for strength to advance in our course; by sincerely saying, “Forgive us, as we also forgive others,” and “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: By this process, it is certainly brought about that our heart is cleansed, and all our sin taken away; and what the righteous King, when sitting on His throne, shall find concealed in the heart and uncleansed as yet, shall be remitted by His mercy, so that the whole shall be rendered sound and cleansed for seeing God.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For “he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath showed no mercy: yet mercy triumpheth against judgment.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: If it were not so, what hope could any of us have?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “When, indeed, the righteous King shall sit upon His throne, who shall boast that he hath a pure heart, or who shall boldly say that he is pure from sin?”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Then, however, through His mercy shall the righteous, being by that time fully and perfectly cleansed, shine forth like the glorious sun in the kingdom of their Father.
